content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of size NNN, we create a graph GGG consisting of NNN vertices by adding the bidirectional edge (i,Ai)(i, A_i)(i,Ai​) for each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.

We say an array is good if the corresponding graph is connected.

Find a rearrangement of the given array AAA such that the rearranged array is good, or output −1-1−1 if no such array exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



if no valid rearrangement exists : just a single integer −1-1−1.
if a valid rearrangement exists : A1′,A2′,…,AN′A'_1, A'_2, \ldots, A'_NA1′​,A2′​,…,AN′​, the rearranged array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The given rearrangement [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] is good because the graph created contains the edges (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) and (3,2)(3, 2)(3,2). [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1] is also a valid output.
